title: clusia subsessilis
intro: Clusia subsessilis, also known as the Clusia tree or the "clueless" Clusia due to its often overlooked nature, is a plant species belonging to the Clusiaceae family. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, leaves, and roots, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Clusia subsessilis include wound healing, fever reduction, and the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, thanks to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds. Herbal preparations of Clusia subsessilis can be made by infusing the bark, leaves, or roots in water to create teas, tinctures, or decoctions for medicinal purposes.
